wins.event: Record Q2 revenue of $46.7B exceeded outlook with sequential growth across all platforms
Q3 revenue outlook of $54B (+$7B sequential) implying continued acceleration
Data center revenue grew 56% YoY despite $4B H20 decline; Blackwell platform grew 17% sequentially
Networking record $7.3B revenue, +46% sequential and +98% YoY across Spectrum-X, InfiniBand, NVLink
$60B share repurchase authorization approved, returned $10B to shareholders in Q2

risks.event: China H20 export controls — Q3 outlook excludes any H20 shipments to China customers
Geopolitical uncertainty between US and China governments continues; license approvals delayed
China declined to low-single-digit percentage of data center revenue this quarter
Inventory increased sequentially from $11B to $15B to support Blackwell/Blackwell Ultra ramp
Hyperscaler ASIC competition (Broadcom, in-house chips) pressuring potential GPU share

guidance.notes: Q3 revenue guided to $54B +/- 2% (over $7B sequential growth); FY non-GAAP gross margin to exit in mid-70s; opex growth raised to high-30s from mid-30s.

_notes: Strong bullish call with one material offsetting risk (China/H20). CEO (Jensen) at peak conviction; CFO more measured given geopolitical overhang. Q&A probing-professional, not hostile.
